news.title: US takes step to halt Nvidia AI chip shipments to Chinese firms outside China
news.summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce on Sunday moved to close a year-old potential loophole it had created that may ‌have led companies to export the world's most advanced chips - like Nvidia's most ‌sophisticated Rubin and Blackwell processors, as well as AMD's MI350x - to Chinese entities located outside China.  The unexpected ​guidance suggests the United States' best AI chips may have been making their way to the subsidiaries of Chinese AI firms based in places like Malaysia for almost a year despite broader U.S. efforts to starve Chinese firms of semiconductors needed to develop critical ‌AI capabilities.  The new guidance was ⁠posted on the Commerce Department's website on Sunday.

news.title: Stock, Oil Futures Set to Trade as Iran War Enters Fourth Month
news.summary: Stock and oil futures are set to begin trading later Sunday, with no progress on the Iran war as it enters its fourth month on Monday.  President Donald Trump told Fox News in an interview that aired Saturday that the U.S. was “close to a very good deal,” but suggested a potential return to fighting as an alternative.  Iran’s Parliament Speaker Mohammad Ghalibaf said Sunday that there would be no deal unless Iran’s rights were secured.
